title: Review & setlist: Bob Dylan was spookily stellar on a smoky night in Boston
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bob Dylan performed at Boston's Leader Bank Pavilion on July 16, 2026, using his distinct voice and newer material.
Dylan's 16-song setlist focused on recent work, featuring songs like 'Man in the Long Black Coat' and 'Tryin' to Get to Heaven'.
Brittney Spencer and Jimmie Vaughan opened for Dylan, with Spencer performing a praised rendition of 'To Make You Feel My Love'.

title: The middle seat will become a table on United’s newest Airbus jets
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines introduces a new concept where middle seats become tables on Airbus A321XLR jets.
The middle-seat-turned-table will be available in Economy Plus, providing extra elbow room and space for belongings.
Domestic flights featuring the new seat design start this fall, with international service planned by early 2027.

title: Original ‘Gilmore Girls’ house is on the market, and it’s not in Stars Hollow
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The original 'Gilmore Girls' house used in the pilot is for sale in Markham, Ontario for $2.76 million USD.
Located at 31 Victoria Ave., the house is a 2,840 sq. ft. Gothic Revival with four bedrooms and four bathrooms.
Built circa 1875, the property includes a 550 sq. ft. barn, originally a village bakery, now zoned for commercial use.

title: Longtime Maine soccer coach recognized by FIFA as ‘Unsung Hero’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Maine coach Gary Newell received FIFA's 'Unsung Hero' award for his contributions to youth soccer.
Newell was honored before the World Cup final at Gillette Stadium, surprising him with a miniature trophy.
England's Jude Bellingham sent a video message to Newell, thanking him for his impact on young players.

title: Decades-old weeping willow collapses in Boston Public Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 68-year-old weeping willow collapsed in Boston Public Garden after exceeding its typical lifespan.
Friends of the Public Garden confirmed routine checks found the willow structurally sound before its fall.
No injuries were reported, and replanting efforts continue after winter tree removals in the Public Garden.

title: These new speed boards in Jamaica Plain, on the Esplanade track cyclists’ speeds, not just cars
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: New speed boards installed by DCR in Jamaica Plain and Charles River Esplanade track speeds of cyclists and vehicles.
The boards aim to reduce cyclist speeds and gather data on path usage, addressing residents' safety concerns.
Installations are part of a broader initiative, with similar boards on Memorial Drive and other state paths.

title: Thousands demand Wu address street safety after cyclist killed in crash
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 4,000 sign letter urging Mayor Wu to enhance street safety after cyclist Louisa Gag's death on Tremont Street.
The letter calls for resuming stalled safety projects, increased transparency, and appointed officials for high-crash areas.
During a vigil, Wu announced enforcement changes and collaboration with advocacy groups for street safety improvements.

title: Police investigating after man, woman in their 70s found dead in Maine home
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Maine State Police are investigating the deaths of Carolyn Harris, 77, and Kevin Harris, 75, found in their West Bath home.
Sagadahoc County deputies discovered the bodies during a welfare check on July 13 on State Road, West Bath.
Autopsies are underway to determine the cause of death; officials state there is no public danger.

title: Multiple issues after getting an SUV back from the body shop
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2024 Honda CR-V EX faced issues after front-end repairs; interior lights and headlights malfunction, hatch locks automatically.
Honda's ADAS systems may need recalibration; body shop's programming technician should recheck vehicle settings post-repair.
Contact insurance for a potential supplemental claim if vehicle features are not restored to pre-collision conditions.

title: Officers shot at during Dorchester traffic stop, police say, prompting outcry from union
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two Boston police officers were shot at during a traffic stop in Dorchester, narrowly escaping injury.
Suspect Rasiel Joel Carbuccia, 20, was apprehended and faces multiple charges including assault and firearm offenses.
The Boston Police Patrolmen’s Association urged officials to condemn the attack, emphasizing officers' safety rights.
